reason:
Missing dependencies. This node sets up the application of the main theorem (Node 1) to the NPA hierarchy, yet it declares no dependencies. It should depend on at least:

(1) Node 1 (main theorem): since the purpose of this node is to describe the NPA feasible set as a constrained state space to which the main theorem applies.

(2) Node 1.6 (matrix algebra recovery): since at any finite level k, the NPA feasible set is concretely an SDP spectrahedron (a convex subset of PSD matrices with linear constraints), and the classical Barvinok-Pataki bound from Node 1.6 applies directly without the *-algebra interpretation.

(3) The definition of NPA_hierarchy and the definition of affinely_constrained_state_space, which are both already in the proof's definition list and are directly used in this node's statement.

Without these dependencies, the logical chain from this node to its parent (1.10) is not formally connected to the rest of the proof.
